items.q: What are James's four characteristics of mystical experience?
items.correct: Ineffability (beyond words), noetic quality (gives knowledge), transience (temporary), and passivity (feeling acted upon by something greater).
items.misconception: Religious experiences are just hallucinations caused by stress.
items.random_wrong: Religious experiences only happen during dreams.
items.why: James was a psychologist describing common features, not assessing truth. Students collapse the question of describing experience into the question of whether God caused it — distinct issues.

items.q: What do orthodox Christians believe about the nature of Jesus?
items.correct: He is fully God and fully human at the same time (the incarnation) — both natures united in one person, as defined at the Council of Chalcedon.
items.misconception: Jesus was half God and half human.
items.random_wrong: Jesus was a human who became God after his resurrection.
items.why: The 'half and half' model was historically rejected as heresy. Orthodox doctrine holds both natures fully and simultaneously — a deliberately paradoxical claim students often soften.

items.q: Is the Buddha a god in Buddhism?
items.correct: No — the Buddha (Siddhartha Gautama) is regarded as an enlightened human teacher who discovered the path to liberation. Theravada Buddhism is generally non-theistic; some Mahayana traditions develop more elaborate cosmologies.
items.misconception: Buddhists worship the Buddha as their god.
items.why: Statues and offerings look like worship to students, but classically the Buddha is venerated as teacher, not worshipped as creator god. The path he taught, not his divinity, is what matters in most Buddhist traditions.

items.q: In which work does Wittgenstein develop the 'language games' approach?
items.why: Correct answer: Philosophical Investigations. “Tractatus Logico-Philosophicus” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: Whose 1965 The Concept of Prayer applies Wittgenstein to religion?
items.why: The answer examiners want is: D.Z. Phillips. “Norman Malcolm” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: In what year were Wittgenstein's Philosophical Investigations published?
items.why: Exact answer: 1953. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
